name: kubernetes
description: Kubernetes cluster management via kubectl. Ask Claude to inspect and manage pods, deployments, services, namespaces, configmaps, secrets, ingresses, and any other Kubernetes resource.

tools.name: kubectl
tools.description:
Run any kubectl command. Pass all arguments after 'kubectl' as an array. Supports stdin for apply -f -.

Examples:
- List pods: ["get", "pods", "-A"]
- Describe deployment: ["describe", "deployment", "my-app", "-n", "prod"]
- Tail logs: ["logs", "pod-name", "--tail=50", "-f"]
- Scale: ["scale", "deployment", "my-app", "--replicas=3"]
- Apply YAML: ["apply", "-f", "-"] with stdin containing YAML
- Get events: ["get", "events", "--sort-by=.lastTimestamp", "-A"]
- Port-forward: not supported (use exec instead)
- Exec into pod: ["exec", "-it", "pod-name", "--", "sh"]

stdin.description: Optional YAML/JSON to pipe to the command (for kubectl apply -f -)
